summary.intent: Fix the `sender_id: None` bug in `channel_binding_from_runtime_context` so that hook payloads carry the correct sender_id, enabling layer 4 group/sender tool policy to work end-to-end.
summary.outcome: Added `channel_sender_id` to `PromptHostRuntimeContext`, populated it from `params["channel"]["sender_id"]` in `apply_request_runtime_context`, and threaded it through `channel_binding_from_runtime_context` and `build_policy_context`. Tests updated and committed.

repo: sender_id in policy resolution reads from `params["channel"]["sender_id"]` (the serialized ChannelMessageMeta), not from the runtime context host — these are two separate code paths.
`channel_binding_from_runtime_context` is only called from `build_tool_context`, which is called from `run_with_tools` — a single call site made the refactor straightforward.

The cleanest way to thread new per-request data to downstream consumers is to add a field to `PromptHostRuntimeContext` and populate it in `apply_request_runtime_context`, rather than changing function signatures throughout the call chain.
The old sandbox policy layer (layer 6) was previously removed because `SandboxConfig` has no `tools_policy` field — it is not wired into the layered policy system yet.
A dedicated `docs/src/tool-policy.md` documenting the full layered system does not yet exist.

code.finding: `PromptHostRuntimeContext` was extended with a `channel_sender_id` field to carry sender_id from the request params down to tool context builders.
`apply_request_runtime_context` now populates `channel_sender_id` from `params["channel"]["sender_id"]`. `channel_binding_from_runtime_context` and `build_policy_context` were updated to consume it.
`build_tool_context` is called from `run_with_tools` at line 7347; params are not directly available there, which is why threading via `PromptHostRuntimeContext` was the chosen approach.

workflow: Parallel agents (docs page + sandbox policy) were launched for independent tasks while a small, targeted code change was handled inline — good parallelism pattern for compound follow-up work.
Checking all call sites with Grep before refactoring a function signature avoids surprise breakage; a single call site confirmed the low blast radius here.
Issue numbers were claimed/created in GitHub before starting work to keep tracking tidy across parallel workstreams.

friction: sender_id was not threaded through the runtime context at all, requiring a multi-file change (prompt.rs + lib.rs) rather than a one-line fix.
params are not available at the `run_with_tools` / `build_tool_context` level, forcing an indirect approach via `PromptHostRuntimeContext` rather than passing params directly.
open_items: Write a dedicated `docs/src/tool-policy.md` page documenting the full layered tool policy system (tracked as a new issue).
Add `tools_policy` field to `SandboxConfig` to re-enable sandbox as layer 6 in the policy stack (tracked as a new issue).
Upstream issue #640 — hook payload `sender_id` population has broader implications beyond this fix.
